# Musing — Sidecar Format Spec

For a document `notes.txt`, the sidecar is `notes.txt.musing.toml`.

## Format

```toml
# Auto-generated by musing. Manual edits to overrides are preserved.
version = 1

# Document-level metadata (optional, user-edited)
[meta]
title = "My Document"
author = "baz"

# Element classifications, keyed by line range
# "5" = single line 5, "10-14" = lines 10 through 14

[elements."1"]
type = "heading"
level = 1

[elements."3-7"]
type = "paragraph"

[elements."9-15"]
type = "code"
language = "python"

[elements."17"]
type = "heading"
level = 2

[elements."19-22"]
type = "list"
style = "unordered"

[elements."24-26"]
type = "blockquote"

[elements."28-35"]
type = "table"
columns = 3

[elements."37-40"]
type = "paragraph"
confidence = 0.6       # heuristics weren't sure — LLM candidate

# User overrides: if present, these take priority over inferred types.
# The plugin never overwrites this section.
[overrides."9-15"]
type = "paragraph"      # user corrected: this isn't code
```

## Element Types

| Type        | Attributes                          |
|-------------|-------------------------------------|
| heading     | level (1-6)                         |
| paragraph   | —                                   |
| list        | style (unordered, ordered, definition) |
| code        | language (optional)                 |
| blockquote  | —                                   |
| table       | columns (int)                       |
| thematic_break | —                                |
| blank       | —                                   |

## Rules

- Line numbers are 1-indexed, matching the editor.
- Every non-blank line in the document should have a classification.
- `confidence` (0.0–1.0) is optional. Below a threshold (default 0.7),
  the line is flagged for LLM review or user attention.
- `[overrides]` section is never touched by the inference engine.
  Users edit this to correct mistakes. Overrides win over `[elements]`.
- `[meta]` section is never touched by the inference engine.
- On re-analysis, `[elements]` is fully regenerated. `[meta]` and
  `[overrides]` are preserved.
